commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=634e1afbfb0a4eda6c2de576868a80479c75544d)
commands.review_summary:
This PR adds a small voice-call greeting-gate helper and handler regressions asserting that queued outbound greetings still trigger explicitly for OpenAI and non-OpenAI realtime providers.

PR surface: Source +8, Tests +132. Total +140 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source, but not live: current main enables triggerGreetingOnReady for queued greetings, session-runtime calls triggerGreeting on ready, OpenAI triggerGreeting sends conversation.item.create plus response.create, and OpenAI server VAD defaults create_response to true. I did not place a live Twilio/OpenAI call, so this is source_reproducible rather than reproduced.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual review is needed because the protected maintainer label is present, the branch does not yet implement a one-response-source fix, and selecting the OpenAI first-turn strategy is a compatibility/product judgment before any repair loop should run.

target.title: fix(voice-call): avoid OpenAI realtime double greeting
target.body:
Fixes #85846

## Summary

- Keep OpenAI outbound queued initial greetings on the active explicit ready-trigger path so the provider still sends `conversation.item.create` followed by `response.create` after `session.updated`.
- Preserve base realtime session instructions instead of moving the queued greeting into `session.update` instructions, addressing the lost-initial-response risk called out in review.
- Keep existing OpenAI manual speech semantics: `triggerGreeting()` still creates a conversation user item and requests a response.
- Keep non-OpenAI realtime providers on the existing explicit on-ready initial greeting path.
- No config surface or plugin API surface changed.

## Behavior proof

The updated focused regression now covers the OpenAI outbound queued-greeting split:

- `createBridge(...).instructions` remains the base session instructions (`"Be helpful."`), so the initial greeting is not passively parked in `session.update`.
- `onReady()` calls `triggerGreeting()` exactly once with the full queued first-turn instruction.
- The existing OpenAI provider regression proves `triggerGreeting()` sends one `conversation.item.create` and one `response.create` after `session.updated`.

## Verification

- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/voice-call/src/webhook/realtime-handler.test.ts extensions/openai/realtime-voice-provider.test.ts --reporter=dot` -> 2 files, 70 tests passed
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check extensions/voice-call/src/webhook/realtime-handler.ts extensions/voice-call/src/webhook/realtime-handler.test.ts extensions/openai/realtime-voice-provider.ts extensions/openai/realtime-voice-provider.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json extensions/voice-call/src/webhook/realtime-handler.ts extensions/voice-call/src/webhook/realtime-handler.test.ts extensions/openai/realtime-voice-provider.ts extensions/openai/realtime-voice-provider.test.ts` -> 0 warnings, 0 errors
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `git diff --check`
- `codex review --base origin/main` -> no actionable regressions found

Signed head: `634e1afbfb0a4eda6c2de576868a80479c75544d`.

Known proof gap: no live Twilio/OpenAI outbound call was run in this environment.
target.author: giodl73-repo

Fixes #85846

## Summary

- Keep OpenAI outbound queued initial greetings on the active explicit ready-trigger path so the provider still sends `conversation.item.create` followed by `response.create` after `session.updated`.
- Preserve base realtime session instructions instead of moving the queued greeting into `session.update` instructions, addressing the lost-initial-response risk called out in review.
- Keep existing OpenAI manual speech semantics: `triggerGreeting()` still creates a conversation user item and requests a response.
- Keep non-OpenAI realtime providers on the existing explicit on-ready initial greeting path.
- No config surface or plugin API surface changed.

## Behavior proof

The updated focused regression now covers the OpenAI outbound queued-greeting split:

- `createBridge(...).instructions` remains the base session instructions (`"Be helpful."`), so the initial greeting is not passively parked in `session.update`.
- `onReady()` calls `triggerGreeting()` exactly once with the full queued first-turn instruction.
- The existing OpenAI provider regression proves `triggerGreeting()` sends one `conversation.item.create` and one `response.create` after `session.updated`.

## Verification

- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/voice-call/src/webhook/realtime-handler.test.ts extensions/openai/realtime-voice-provider.test.ts --reporter=dot` -> 2 files, 70 tests passed
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check extensions/voice-call/src/webhook/realtime-handler.ts extensions/voice-call/src/webhook/realtime-handler.test.ts extensions/openai/realtime-voice-provider.ts extensions/openai/realtime-voice-provider.test.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxlint --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json extensions/voice-call/src/webhook/realtime-handler.ts extensions/voice-call/src/webhook/realtime-handler.test.ts extensions/openai/realtime-voice-provider.ts extensions/openai/realtime-voice-provider.test.ts` -> 0 warnings, 0 errors
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD`
- `git diff --check`
- `codex review --base origin/main` -> no actionable regressions found

Signed head: `634e1afbfb0a4eda6c2de576868a80479c75544d`.

Known proof gap: no live Twilio/OpenAI outbound call was run in this environment.
